summary: Matched or made equivalent the melee/lb/lbarchive.c object by rewriting archive-loading helpers, correcting prototypes and const/size types, adding several previously missing lbArchive functions, and updating baselib archive APIs accordingly. configure.py moved lbarchive.c from NonMatching to Equivalent. The PR also split a previously broad data symbol at 0x803BA5B0 into two local string labels, improving symbol accuracy.
what_changed: Changed configure.py classification for melee/lb/lbarchive.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Equivalent, ...).
Updated lbArchive_InitializeDAT to take void* data and size_t length instead of u8* and u32, and changed local symbol pointers to const char*.
Reworked lbArchive_LoadSections and related varargs loaders to use clearer symbol/symbol_name naming and const char* symbol names.
Introduced static inline helpers lbArchive_LoadArchive_inline, lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal, and lbArchive_vLoadSections to share code while preserving matching behavior.
Changed lbArchive_LoadArchive, lbArchive_LoadSymbols, and lbArchive_80016DBC signatures to return HSD_Archive* and take const char* filenames.
Added implementations for lbArchive_80016F80, lbArchive_80017040, lbArchive_800171CC, and lbArchive_80017340.
Added lbdvd dependency in lbarchive.c and used lbDvd_8001819C to detect preloaded archives before falling back to loading from file.
Replaced manual 32-byte rounding expression with OSRoundUp32B(lbFile_800163D8(filename)) for archive allocation sizing.
Updated baselib archive prototypes and definitions: HSD_ArchiveParse now uses size_t file_size, HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress takes const char*, and HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern takes const char*.

Added relocation/parsing logic in lbArchive_80017340 that initializes an HSD_Archive from an HSD_ArchiveHeader, validates file size, computes data/relocation/public/extern/symbol table pointers, and applies base-address relocation.
Adjusted symbols.txt by replacing lbArchive_803BA5B0 as a single 0x40 global object with two local string labels: lbl_803BA5B0 and lbl_803BA5C8.
smart_moves: Factored repeated varargs archive symbol lookup loops into inline helpers with fatal and nonfatal variants, matching the different assertion behavior while reducing duplicated source structure.
Used const char* for filenames and archive symbol names, matching read-only string usage and propagating the type correction through both lbarchive and baselib archive APIs.
Used size_t for file sizes in archive parsing/loading paths, aligning prototypes with size semantics and avoiding narrower u32-only signatures where the diff indicates size values are passed around.
Kept a small inline wrapper around lbArchive_LoadArchive instead of directly inlining all callers, likely to influence code generation while keeping a clean external symbol.
Used OSRoundUp32B instead of an open-coded alignment expression, making the allocation intent clearer while preserving the 32-byte rounding behavior.
Added explicit preloaded-archive paths through lbDvd_8001819C for functions that return whether an archive was already available.
Inserted small unused stack byte arrays in some functions, an evidence-backed matching tactic likely used to reproduce stack layout.
Split the 0x803BA5B0 data symbol into two string labels, avoiding an over-broad global object when evidence indicated separate strings.
decomp_lessons: When matching archive loader code, varargs prototypes must match the actual calling convention closely; the first fixed parameter and va_start target are especially important.
For varargs symbol loaders, separate fatal and nonfatal lookup behavior can be represented with shared inline helpers if it helps reproduce codegen while keeping behavior clear.
Const-correcting string parameters should be propagated through headers and dependent baselib functions to prevent mismatched declarations.
File-size and allocation-size types matter for matching and readability; size_t is preferable where the source treats values as sizes.
Replacing hand-written bitmask alignment with a known macro such as OSRoundUp32B can both document intent and match expected compiler output.
Data symbol boundaries in symbols.txt should be revisited when decompilation reveals embedded strings; a single guessed global object may need to become multiple lbl_* string entries.
Functions that load resources may have a preloaded-DVD-cache branch; checking lbdvd before heap allocation can be structurally important for matching.

Relocation routines should reconstruct archive internal pointers from the header layout in order: data, reloc_info, public_info, extern_info, symbols, then apply base-address additions to relocation offsets.
naming_conventions: Used lbArchive_* prefix for melee library archive functions.
Kept unknown functions named by address, such as lbArchive_80016DBC, lbArchive_80016F80, lbArchive_80017040, lbArchive_800171CC, and lbArchive_80017340.
Named varargs pointee parameters symbol or symbols and string values symbol_name, clarifying the alternating pointer/name argument pattern.
Used const char* filename for archive file path parameters.
Used HSD_Archive* and HSD_ArchiveHeader* in prototypes rather than generic void* where types were known.
Used lbl_803BA5B0 and lbl_803BA5C8 for local string data labels when no semantic name was established.
Named internal helpers with descriptive suffixes: lbArchive_LoadArchive_inline, lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal, and lbArchive_vLoadSections.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked lbarchive.c as Equivalent rather than Matching in configure.py, indicating the PR improved decompilation quality to equivalent codegen/behavior but not necessarily byte-identical matching.
Used #pragma dont_inline around lbArchive_InitializeDAT and introduced static inline helpers to steer inlining decisions.
Introduced unused stack padding arrays such as u8 _[8] in several functions, likely to match stack frame layout.
Used OSRoundUp32B for heap allocation size to match the original 32-byte alignment pattern.
Separated fatal and nonfatal varargs lookup loops so the HSD_ASSERT path appears only in functions that require fatal missing-symbol behavior.
Changed broad data symboling to exact string labels, which can affect rodata/data matching and symbol map correctness.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no comment body was included in the excerpt, so reviewer intent or discussion cannot be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename lbArchive_80016DBC, lbArchive_80016F80, lbArchive_80017040, lbArchive_800171CC, or lbArchive_80017340 with semantic names.
Search for callers of lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC to confirm the meaning of the returned bool as preloaded/archive-from-DVD-cache.
Compare future changes to HSD_ArchiveParse and lbArchive_80017340 for additional struct layout or relocation field naming improvements.
Check whether lbarchive.c eventually moves from Equivalent to Matching and what remaining codegen differences were resolved.
Search for similar varargs symbol-loading patterns in other lb or baselib files that can reuse the fatal/nonfatal inline helper approach.
